raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a {$150.00} XXXX gift card at XXXX using my American Express card. When I gave the card to the recipient, they attempted to use it but were told by XXXX that the card had already been redeemed online before they ever received it. I contacted XXXX, who confirmed it had been used but said they could not assist since they werent the seller. I contacted XXXX, who also refused to assist and told me to dispute the charge with my credit card company. 

I submitted a formal dispute with American Express for a charge involving a fraudulently used gift card. Despite providing all relevant details and evidence, AmEx sided with XXXX and closed the dispute without issuing a credit. This decision disregards the fact that I paid for a product ( a gift card ) that had already been compromised and used by someone else. This is a clear case of consumer fraud. Neither the merchant nor the issuer is taking responsibility, and I am being unfairly held liable for a product I never received the benefit of.

Im requesting CFPB intervention to compel American Express to reopen the dispute and issue a refund. I am also concerned that XXXX XXXX have sold other compromised gift cards and that this situation could be affecting more consumers.
